wiki_scope_head: Title: Scopes - Europa Universalis 4 Wiki
URL Source: http://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Scope
Published Time: Thu, 05 Mar 2026 04:46:22 GMT
Markdown Content:
Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified for [version](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Europa_Universalis_4_Wiki:Versioning "Europa Universalis 4 Wiki:Versioning") 1.23.
A **scope** is the context that [triggers](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Triggers "Triggers") and [effects](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Effects "Effects") are used in.

Overview[[edit](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview") | [edit source](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]
All script scopes operate on one of two internal scopes: _country_ and _province_. Scopes that are for a country scope will not function correctly when used in a province scope.

Additionally, all script scopes operate in one of two type scopes: _effect_ and _trigger_. Using an effect scope within a trigger scope will not work, and vice versa. There are scopes that operate in both type scopes.

Which base scopes are _effect_ and which are _trigger_ depends on the feature you are modding. For example, in a decision: `potential = { }` and `allow = { }` are _trigger_ scopes, whereas `effect = { }` is an _effect_ scope. This is normally self-evident in most cases.

### Dynamic scopes[[edit](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Dynamic scopes") | [edit source](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Dynamic scopes")]
Four script scopes are unique in that they change their contents depending on where they are used. They can be used in both _effect_ and _trigger_ scopes.
| Scope | Description | Example | Notes |
| ROOT | The base scope. | The ROOT scope in a country event is the country that the event fires for. |  |
| FROM | The calling scope. | The FROM scope in a country event would be the ROOT scope of another event that called it (via the `country_event = {}` effect). |  |

| PREV | The previous scope. | If you change the scope to `FRA = { }` and then to change scope to `capital_scope = { }` within it, the PREV scope is `FRA = { }`. | There is also "PREV_PREV", although this only works as target for the province_id trigger. |

| THIS | The current scope. | If you change scope to `FRA = { }` and then the THIS scope is `FRA = { }` while used within the `FRA = { }` scope. | This only works as a target for an effect. Effects placed within a THIS scope will not work correctly. |

### Logic scopes[[edit](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Logic scopes") | [edit source](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Logic scopes")]
There are three trigger scopes used to alter the boolean value of a trigger, or set of triggers. They are _AND_, _OR_ and _NOT_.

#### AND[[edit](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: AND") | [edit source](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Scopes&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: AND")]

The _AND_ scope groups a set of triggers together, and returns true only if all triggers nested within return true as well. This scope is the default scope for all trigger scopes, so you don't need to explicitly use a _AND_ scope most of the time.

builtin_triggers.name: absolutism
notes: Returns true if the country has at least X absolutism.

builtin_triggers.name: accepted_culture
notes: Returns true if the country accepts the specified culture.

builtin_triggers.name: active_imperial_incident
notes: Checks whether a given (or any) imperial incident is currently active.

builtin_triggers.name: active_major_mission
notes: Returns true if X is the current mission of the country.

builtin_triggers.name: adm
notes: Returns true if the country has a ruler with administrative skill of at least X, or at least as high military skill as the ruler of the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: adm_power
notes: Returns true if the country has at least X administrative power, or at least as much as the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: adm_power_cost
notes: Multiplies the value by all_power_cost modifiers

builtin_triggers.name: adm_tech
notes: Returns true if the country has an administrative technology of at least X, or at least as high as the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: advisor
notes: Returns true if the country has an advisor of the specified type.

builtin_triggers.name: advisor_exists
notes: Returns true if the advisor X exists.

builtin_triggers.name: ai
notes: Returns true if the country is controlled by the AI.

builtin_triggers.name: ai_attitude
notes: Returns true if the country has the specified attitude towards country X.

builtin_triggers.name: all_regiments_morale_percent
notes: Checks regiments morale percentage

builtin_triggers.name: allows_female_emperor

builtin_triggers.name: always
notes: Returns true under all circumstances if set to yes, returns false under all circumstances if set to no.

builtin_triggers.name: area
notes: Returns true if the province is part of the area X. Accepts province scope as target.

builtin_triggers.name: army_balance
notes: Returns true if the country's army size is at least the size of X times the army size of the specified country (done by number of regiments)

builtin_triggers.name: army_professionalism
notes: Returns true if the country has at least X army professionalism, or at least as much as the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: army_size

builtin_triggers.name: army_size_percentage
notes: Returns true if the total army size of the country is at least X% of its land force limit.

builtin_triggers.name: army_strength
notes: Returns true if the country's army size is at least the size of X times the army size of the specified country (done by army strength, somehow)

builtin_triggers.name: army_tradition
notes: Returns true if the country has an army tradition of at least X, or at least as high as the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: artillery_fraction
notes: Returns true if the ratio of the artillery fraction to the army size of the country is at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: artillery_in_province
notes: Returns true if there are at least X artillery units in the province, or if there are artillery units from the specified country in the province.

builtin_triggers.name: at_war_with_religious_enemy

builtin_triggers.name: authority
notes: Returns true if the country has at least X authority, or if the country has at least as much authority as the specified country.

builtin_triggers.name: average_autonomy
notes: Returns true if the country has an average autonomy in its provinces of at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: average_autonomy_above_min
notes: TODO: Needs description

builtin_triggers.name: average_effective_unrest
notes: TODO: Needs description

builtin_triggers.name: average_home_autonomy
notes: Returns true if the country has an average autonomy in its provinces excluding overseas provinces is at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: average_unrest
notes: Returns true if the country has an average unrest in its provinces of at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: base_manpower
notes: Returns true if the base manpower of the province is at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: base_production
notes: Returns true if the base production of the province is at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: base_tax
notes: Returns true if the base tax of the province is at least X.

builtin_triggers.name: blockade

builtin_triggers.name: can_heir_be_child_of_consort

builtin_triggers.name: church_power

builtin_triggers.name: coalition_target

builtin_triggers.name: country_or_vassal_holds
notes: Returns true if the province is part of the specified country or its subjects.

builtin_effects.name: Name
notes: Description

Adds absolutism to the current scope. Can also subtract. No effect if Absolutism isn't active.

Adds a new accepted culture to the current scope. Cultures are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/cultures/*.txt.

builtin_effects.name: add_active_policy
notes: Adds a policy for the current scope.

Adds a policy for the current scope. Policies are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/policies/*.txt. Won't work if the policy triggers are not met by the country, or there are no free policy slots.

builtin_effects.name: add_adm_power
notes: Adds Administrative power to the current scope.
Adds [administrative power](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Administrative_power "Administrative power") to the current scope.

notes: Adds ADM technologies to the current scope. You cannot remove technologies.
Adds _ADM_ technologies to the current scope.

The current country scope adds X amount of aggressive expansion towards the specified country.

notes: Adds Army Professionalism to the current scope.
Adds Army Professionalism to the current scope. 1 is equal to 100 professionalism.

builtin_effects.name: add_army_tradition
notes: Adds Army Tradition to the current scope.

Adds authority to the current scope. Used in Religious Reforms that use authority. No effect if the country does not hold a religion using authority.

notes: Adds base manpower to the current province scope.

notes: Starts building construction in the current province scope.

notes: Adds a Cardinal to the current province scope, for the current owner.

Increases the level of a center of trade in the province. A negative number decreases the level of the center of trade.

Adds the defined aspect to the current scope. Church Aspects are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/church_aspects/00_church_aspects.txt. No effect if the country does not hold a religion using uses_church_power and the aspect set in aspects. Does not work with Blessings.

builtin_effects.name: add_church_power
notes: Adds Church Power to the current scope.
Adds Church Power to the current scope. No effect if the country does not hold a religion using uses_church_power.

The defined scope gains a claim on the current province scope.

notes: Adds settlers to the current province scope if a colony.
Adds settlers to the current province scope of a colony.

Changes the defined Mercenary Company's manpower by x percent.

Changes the construction progress of any construction in the current province scope.

The defined scope gains a core on the current province scope.

Starts core construction in the current province scope, for the current owner. Second instance starts core construction at double the speed and full coring cost.

Adds corruption to the current scope. Can also subtract.

Adds modifier to the current scope as a country modifier.

builtin_effects.name: convert_female_heir_to_general
notes: Converts a female heir into a general.

builtin_effects.name: convert_female_ruler_to_general
notes: Converts a female ruler into a general.

builtin_effects.name: convert_heir_to_general
notes: Converts a male heir into a general.

builtin_effects.name: convert_leader_to_general
notes: Mercenary leader becomes regular general

builtin_effects.name: convert_ruler_to_admiral

Converts a male ruler into an admiral. If an integer is given then it is used as [navy tradition](https://eu4.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_tradition "Navy tradition") during generation.

builtin_effects.name: convert_ruler_to_general

notes: Spawns a cossack cavalry unit in the current province scope for the defined country scope.
Spawns a cossack cavalry unit in the current province scope for the defined country scope. Uses the currently selected cavalry unit.

builtin_effects.name: cossack_infantry
notes: Spawns a cossack infantry unit in the current province scope for the defined country scope.
Spawns a cossack infantry unit in the current province scope for the defined country scope. Uses the currently selected infantry unit.

builtin_effects.name: country_event
notes: Fire a country event for the current country scope.
Fire a country event for the current country scope. You cannot fire the same event within itself.

builtin_effects.name: create_admiral
notes: Generates an new admiral for the current scope.
Generates an new admiral for the current scope. The number is the amount of navy tradition to use in generation.

builtin_effects.name: create_advisor
notes: Adds the defined advisor for the owner of the current province scope.
Adds the defined advisor to the current scope.

In country scope, adds the defined advisor to the current scope. Defaults the advisor's location to the capital, and religion and culture to the current scope's. In province scope, adds the defined advisor for the owner of the current province scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_alliance
notes: Creates an alliance between the current scope and the defined scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_colony
notes: Starts a colony belonging to the owner of the ROOT province scope.
Starts a colony belonging to the owner of the ROOT province scope. Use with e.g. random_empty_neighbor_province

builtin_effects.name: create_conquistador
notes: Generates an new conquistador for the current scope.
Generates an new conquistador for the current scope. The number is the amount of army tradition to use in generation.

builtin_effects.name: create_explorer
notes: Generates an new explorer for the current scope.
Generates an new explorer for the current scope. The number is the amount of navy tradition to use in generation.

builtin_effects.name: create_flagship
notes: Spawns a flagship unit for the current scope in the defined province scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_general
notes: Generates an new general for the current scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_guarantee
notes: Creates a guarantee over the defined scope for the current scope.
Creates an guarantee over the defined scope for the current scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_independent_estate
notes: Creates an independent nation from among the current scope's owned provinces.

Creates an independent nation from provinces owned by the specified estate within the current scope's owned provinces. Estates are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/estates/*.txt. The additional parameters for the independent nation are found within an estate's file.

builtin_effects.name: create_independent_estate_from_religion
notes: Creates an independent nation from among the current scope's owned provinces.

Creates an independent nation from provinces owned by the specified estate within the current scope's owned provinces. Estates are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/estates/*.txt. Created with the religion that the majority of the provinces that the defined estate own holds. The additional parameters for the independent nation are found within an estate's file.

builtin_effects.name: create_march
notes: Makes the defined scope a march subject of the current scope.
Makes the defined scope a march subject of the current scope. Uses the march subject type.

builtin_effects.name: create_marriage
notes: Creates a royal marriage between the current scope and the defined scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_named_ship
notes: Name of the flagship, defaults to a random culture one

builtin_effects.name: create_native
notes: Spawns (native population * multiplier) natives in the current province scope, rounded up to nearest 1k stack.
Spawns natives in the current province scope. Only useful in colonies; will immediately disappear in cities or uncolonized provinces.

builtin_effects.name: create_pirate
notes: Spawns pirates in the current province scope.
Spawns pirates in the current province scope. Only spawnable in sea zones and land provinces with ports.

builtin_effects.name: create_revolt
notes: Spawns the most likely rebel type in the current province scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_subject

Makes the defined scope into the defined subject type under the current scope. Subject types are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/subject_types/*.txt.

builtin_effects.name: create_union
notes: Makes the defined scope a junior partner in a personal union with the current scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_unit_forcelimit_percentage
notes: Builds the defined units as a percentage of the owner's forcelimit in capital of the current country scope.

builtin_effects.name: create_vassal
notes: Creates the defined vassal out of the scopes territory if possible.
Makes the defined scope a vassal subject of the current scope. Uses the vassal subject type.

builtin_effects.name: custom_tooltip
notes: Displays a localized key in the effect tooltip.

builtin_effects.name: declare_war
notes: Creates a no casus belli war against the defined scope for the current scope.

Declares war for the current scope against the defined scope, using the defined casus belli. Casus belli are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/cb_types/*.txt

builtin_effects.name: decolonize
notes: Decolonizes the scoped province, making it unowned
Decolonizes the specified province, making it unowned
The current scope is decolonized.

builtin_effects.name: define_advisor
notes: Adds the defined advisor to the current scope.

Adds the defined advisor to the current scope. Advisor types are found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/advisortypes/*.txt. Can utilise Event Scope Values for the culture and religion parameters. The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.

builtin_effects.name: define_conquistador
notes: Creates a conquistador with the defined attributes for the current scope.

Creates a conquistador with the defined attributes for the current scope. Leader traits can be found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/leader_personalities/*.txt. The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.

builtin_effects.name: define_consort
notes: Creates the defined consort for the current scope.
Creates the defined consort for the current scope. The skill values are not rolled for consorts, meaning the values set are the exact values a consort gets.

builtin_effects.name: define_exiled_ruler
notes: Defines a new exiled ruler, saving them to the unique string.

builtin_effects.name: define_explorer
notes: Creates an explorer with the defined attributes for the current scope.

Creates an explorer with the defined attributes for the current scope. Leader traits can be found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/leader_personalities/*.txt. Siege improves Blockade Efficiency for explorers. The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.

builtin_effects.name: define_general
notes: Creates a general with the defined attributes for the current scope.

Creates a general with the defined attributes for the current scope. Leader traits can be found in /Europa Universalis IV/common/leader_personalities/*.txt. The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.

builtin_effects.name: define_heir
notes: Creates the defined heir for the current scope.

Creates the defined heir for the current scope. The culture and religion attributes can utilise Event Scope Values. The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.

notes: Creates a general from the heir of the current scope.

The name parameter can accept a saved name variable, see set_saved_name for more context.  If dynasty = historic_dynasty is used the game will pick one weighted by how many rulers a family has had.

builtin_effects.name: define_ruler_to_general

the current country scope disbands all troops it has. No matter where they are. Units, which are in battle while this effect fires are "stack wiped".
